description: Add a validate job to .github/workflows/release.yml that gates the Docker build/push and GitHub Release on the same checks CI runs (lint, format check, build, test, root scripts tests), and reconcile the context/rules/git.md doc claim to match what actually runs.

userStories.title: Add a validate job that gates the publish job in release.yml
userStories.description: As the maintainer, I want the release workflow to run the same lint/build/test checks as CI before it publishes anything, so a broken commit on a tag can never ship an image to GHCR.
acceptanceCriteria: release.yml defines a new job 'validate' (runs-on: ubuntu-latest) with permissions: contents: read only

validate job steps mirror ci-harness.yml's ci job exactly, in order: Checkout (actions/checkout@v4), Setup Node 22.x (actions/setup-node@v4, node-version '22'), Install pnpm (pnpm/action-setup@v4), Cache pnpm store (actions/cache@v4, path: ~/.local/share/pnpm/store/v3, key pnpm-store-${{ hashFiles('pnpm-lock.yaml') }}, restore-keys pnpm-store-), Install (pnpm install --frozen-lockfile), Lint (pnpm --filter './packages/**' -r --if-present run lint), Format check (format:check), Build (build), Test (test), Root tests (pnpm test:scripts)
The cache step includes the explicit path: ~/.local/share/pnpm/store/v3 (omitting it causes a cache miss every run)
The existing publish job (name 'Build & Release') declares needs: [validate]; verifiable via grep -A2 'name: Build & Release' .github/workflows/release.yml showing needs referencing validate
The top-level workflow permissions block is removed; contents: write + packages: write are moved onto the publish job body (grep-verifiable on the job, not just the file)
The publish job keeps its OWN Setup Node + Install pnpm steps (jobs do not share runner state); the apparent duplication is expected and must not be deduplicated
No publish-side steps are removed or reordered: tag trigger, Parse tag, Extract release notes, Setup Node, Install pnpm, Buildx, GHCR login, Build & push, Create GitHub Release all remain
release.yml parses as valid YAML: python3 -c "import yaml; yaml.safe_load(open('.github/workflows/release.yml'))" exits 0
userStories.notes: Completed 2026-06-11 via /delegate; validate job mirrors ci-harness.yml (10 steps byte-identical), needs:[validate], permissions split.

userStories.title: Reconcile the git.md release description
userStories.description: As the maintainer, I want context/rules/git.md to accurately describe what the release workflow runs, so the docs match reality.
acceptanceCriteria: PROTECTED-PATH override: this is a documentation correction (not a deletion/deprecation) of context/rules/git.md (== .claude/rules/git.md via symlink, protected-paths:50); permitted under the deletion-only gate
The phrase 'runs lint + type-check + tests' on context/rules/git.md:130 is replaced with wording matching the actual gate (lint, format check, build, test, root-scripts tests)
The string 'type-check' no longer appears on that line; no type-check script is invented
The surrounding sentence still reads correctly (tag push triggers release.yml, which now validates then builds/pushes to GHCR and creates a GitHub Release)
The edit is made to the canonical file context/rules/git.md (never the symlink); diff context/rules/git.md .claude/rules/git.md exits 0 afterward
userStories.notes: Completed 2026-06-11 via /delegate; git.md:130 reworded (type-check removed), symlink diff clean.

userStories.title: Add the CHANGELOG entry
userStories.description: As the maintainer, I want the release-gate change recorded in the changelog so it surfaces in the next release notes.
acceptanceCriteria: A new bullet exists under ## [Unreleased] -> ### Changed: 'Release pipeline now validates (lint, format check, build, test, root tests) before building/pushing the Docker image or creating the GitHub Release ([#24](https://github.com/ryaneggz/openharness/issues/24)).'
The bullet links issue #24 using the ryaneggz/openharness namespace (matches all current [Unreleased] links)
No existing [Unreleased] entries are removed or reordered
